description: Known weighted-survey datasets. Used by survey-weights-check.sh (G1). When data/data-status.json.dataset_id matches dataset_id below AND design-blueprint.json.measurement_strategy.weights_policy ∈ {apply_published_weights, apply_constructed_weights}, the analysis script must invoke svyglm/svydesign/lm-with-weights/feols-with-weights. Adding a dataset is a JSON edit — no code change required.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~stratum, weights = ~rswt_natcs20, data = df, nest = TRUE)
datasets.notes: Wave-specific weight variable name; use respondent-level cross-section weight for cross-section, household-level for HH analysis.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~vpsu, strata = ~vstrat, weights = ~wtssall, data = df, nest = TRUE)
datasets.notes: wtssall recommended for cross-year analyses; wtss for single-year. Stratum/PSU available 2002+; pre-2002 requires srs approximation.

datasets.example_svydesign: svrepdesign(weights = ~pwgtp, repweights = paste0('pwgtp', 1:80), type = 'JK1', scale = 4/80, rscales = rep(1, 80), data = df)
datasets.notes: Use replicate-weight design (Jackknife 1); 80 replicate weights pwgtp1..pwgtp80.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~hrhhid, strata = ~gestfips, weights = ~earnwt, data = df, nest = TRUE)
datasets.notes: Different weight per rotation (earnwt for MORG earnings, asecwt for ASEC supplement, marsupwt for March supplement).

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~psu, strata = ~stratum, weights = ~anweight, data = df, nest = TRUE)
datasets.notes: anweight is the analysis weight (post-stratification × design × population size). Use for cross-country comparisons.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~w_weight, data = df)
datasets.notes: Country-level post-stratification weights; PSU/stratum often absent in public files.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~weight_g, data = df)
datasets.notes: weight_g is the global weight (post-stratification); weight_d is design weight.

datasets.long_name: OECD Programme for International Student Assessment
datasets.example_svydesign: svrepdesign(weights = ~w_fstuwt, repweights = paste0('w_fstr', 1:80), type = 'Fay', rho = 0.5, data = df)
datasets.notes: Use Fay BRR replicates (rho=0.5) per PISA technical manual.

datasets.long_name: Trends in International Mathematics and Science Study
datasets.example_svydesign: svrepdesign(weights = ~totwgt, repweights = paste0('jkrep', 1:75), type = 'JK1', data = df)
datasets.notes: Use jackknife replicate weights for variance estimation.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~v021, strata = ~v022, weights = ~I(v005/1000000), data = df, nest = TRUE)
datasets.notes: Weights stored ×1,000,000; divide by 1e6 before use. v005=women's, hv005=households, mv005=men's.

datasets.long_name: National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey
datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~sdmvpsu, strata = ~sdmvstra, weights = ~wtmec2yr, data = df, nest = TRUE)
datasets.notes: wtmec for MEC-examined sample; wtint for interviewed sample. Multiply weights when combining cycles (2-yr → 4-yr → etc.).

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~1, strata = ~stratum, weights = ~fwc, data = df)
datasets.notes: fwc is the child-level final weight. Stratum used for variance estimation.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~1, strata = ~x_ststr, weights = ~x_llcpwt, data = df)
datasets.notes: x_llcpwt is the final iterative-raking weight.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~secu, strata = ~stratum, weights = ~pwgtr, data = df, nest = TRUE)
datasets.notes: pwgtr = person-level respondent weight; hwgthh = household-level. Wave-specific.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~varpsu, strata = ~varstr, weights = ~perwt, data = df, nest = TRUE)
datasets.notes: perwt = person-level final weight.

datasets.long_name: National Longitudinal Study of Adolescent to Adult Health
datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~psuscid, strata = ~region, weights = ~gswgt5, data = df, nest = TRUE)
datasets.notes: Wave-specific grand-sample weights; gswgt5 for Wave V cross-section.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~w1, data = df)
datasets.notes: w1 = country-level post-stratification weight; wnation = combined cross-EU weight.

datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~withinwt, data = df)
datasets.notes: withinwt = country-level; combinwt = cross-country.

datasets.example_svydesign: svrepdesign(weights = ~perwt, repweights = paste0('repwtp', 1:80), type = 'JK1', scale = 4/80, rscales = rep(1, 80), data = df)
datasets.notes: Use replicate weights when extracting from ACS samples; otherwise strata/cluster pair.

datasets.long_name: Survey of Health, Ageing and Retirement in Europe
datasets.example_svydesign: svydesign(ids = ~1, weights = ~cciw_w8, data = df)
datasets.notes: Wave-specific cross-sectional individual weight (cciw_wN) and longitudinal weight (ciw_wN).
